dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, {target_npc} found out. They know it was me who ran.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Someone recognized me and told {target_npc} everything about {quest_origin}. They came shaking with anger and demanded to know why. I told them the truth. They said, 'Then prove you've changed.' They want me to help defend {location} personally. But first, more {quest_item} from {location_hint}, and the {enemy_type} are thicker than ever. {quest_threat} no longer lets me hide.
dialogueChunks.outro: I asked for a chance to make amends and got the hard kind. {quest_focus} demands I stop running. Will you stand with me?

playerResponses.text: You asked for this. Now earn it. I'll stand with you.
Proving yourself under fire. That's the only language that matters.
Your redemption arc isn't my problem.

dialogueChunks.intro: The medicine we gathered, {player_name}... it's been stolen.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Bandits raided the supply wagon {location_hint}. Everything: every vial of {quest_item} meant to {quest_action}. {target_npc} doesn't even know the shipment was coming from me. {quest_stakes} becomes real again if I can't replace it. The bandits left a trail through {enemy_type} territory, and the supplies might still be recoverable.
dialogueChunks.outro: The universe has a cruel sense of humor. But {quest_focus} drives me forward. I'm not quitting.

playerResponses.text: Then we get it back. Every last drop.
Stolen medicine in enemy territory. Your redemption just got expensive.
Fate seems to be telling you something. Maybe listen.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}... {target_npc} came to the memorial site while I was building it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: He saw the half-finished monument to his daughter and broke down. I tried to leave, but he caught my arm and asked why a stranger was honoring his child. He read {quest_origin} right off my face. He walked away without a word. Then {enemy_type} tore through the site last night, scattering the {quest_item}. {quest_threat} is crushing, but I have to finish. For her, not for me.
dialogueChunks.outro: {quest_focus} doesn't need to be about my peace. She deserved to be remembered.

playerResponses.text: For her, then. Let's rebuild it right.
You owe him the truth in words, not just stones. But I'll help with the stones.
Some wounds heal. Some don't. I can't tell you which this is.
